world.summary: The new Labour leader says his government will deliver the biggest change in British politics for 40 years.

business.title: US student visa rule changes to raise costs, tighten timelines for international students
business.summary: Students will need to plan careers earlier as visa extensions become more complex

business.title: The New Oil? Why the World Is Chasing Copper
business.summary: Copper has emerged as a strategic commodity powering the global shift towards clean energy, electric vehicles and digital infrastructure. With demand rising faster than supply due to electrification and constrained mining output, the metal is increasingly viewed as a critical resource for the global economy's long-term transition.

business.title: Concurrent Gainers: 13 smallcap stocks that gain for 5 days in a row
business.summary: Thirteen BSE SmallCap stocks rallied 10-15% after posting gains in each of the last five trading sessions, outperforming the broader market's modest advance.

business.title: Agentic AI, alternative data and SIFs take centre stage at Indian Institutional Quant Conference 2026
business.summary: Agentic AI, alternative data and Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) dominated discussions at the Indian Institutional Quant Conference 2026, where industry leaders, academics and policymakers explored the evolving role of quantitative investing, AI-driven strategies and regulatory developments shaping India's institutional investment landscape.

business.title: Xtranet Technologies announces IPO dates for launch next week. Check details
business.summary: Xtranet Technologies will launch its Rs 166.8 crore IPO on July 23 at a price band of Rs 120-127 per share. The issue, comprising a fresh issue of 1.31 crore shares, will fund debt repayment, capital expenditure and working capital, with listing scheduled on July 30.

business.title: Longer-dated yields lower after data, on track for weekly decline
business.summary: Longer-dated U.S. Treasury yields declined on Friday after recent economic data. Markets largely priced out a Federal Reserve rate hike for this month. Import prices increased unexpectedly, while consumer and producer prices showed moderation. Geopolitical worries and hawkish Fed comments added to market uncertainty. Bond yields moved lower, suggesting a flight to safety amid rising oil prices.

business.title: Czech government cuts bond issuance plan after strong demand for retail bonds
business.summary: Czech Finance Ministry will reduce planned bond issuance by 130 billion crowns. This decision follows strong demand for retail bonds offered recently. Overall gross borrowing needs for 2026 remain largely unchanged. Two more retail bond offerings are scheduled for later this year. This comes after 74 billion crowns sold in the first round.

business.title: Oil settles up on renewed US-Iran hostilities and threat of Red Sea closure
business.summary: Oil prices climbed significantly on Friday, reaching their highest point in over a month. Escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran intensified attacks across the Gulf region. Shipping routes face potential closure, impacting global oil flows and prices. Saudi Arabia has redirected significant crude exports to avoid the Strait of Hormuz. Ukraine also reported striking a Russian oil refinery in its conflict.

business.title: CJP founder Abhijit Dipke begins indefinite hunger strike after Wangchuk shifted to hospital
business.summary: Dipke’s announcement comes amid continued protests at Jantar Mantar, with AISA activists Neha, Aameen and Manish also carrying on with their hunger strike after Wangchuk’s hospitalisation

business.title: Gold set for biggest weekly drop since early June on inflation, rate-hike worries
business.summary: Gold prices saw an increase on Friday, driven by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. These geopolitical events fueled energy prices and inflation concerns, impacting market sentiment. Higher inflation fears reinforced expectations of potential U.S. interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. While gold is a hedge against inflation, higher rates typically pressure the non-yielding metal. Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also experienced weekly losses.

business.title: Tech selloff weighs on European shares ahead of ECB meeting next week
business.summary: European stocks edged lower Friday, influenced by a global tech sell-off and Middle East tensions. Investors await the European Central Bank meeting and corporate earnings reports next week. Rising oil prices and subdued reactions to strong results complicated the market backdrop. Tech stocks experienced a significant decline this week, impacting major AI beneficiaries. Utilities and defense sectors saw gains, while luxury brands faced headwinds from reduced tourist spending.

business.title: IPO calendar: 5 IPOs opening for subscription to keep investors busy; SBI Funds among 4 listings scheduled
business.summary: The IPO market will remain active next week with five public issues opening for subscription, including mainboard offerings from Xtranet Technologies and Cube Highways Trust InvIT. Investors will also watch four listings, led by SBI Funds Management, as strong demand and healthy grey market premiums keep primary market sentiment buoyant.

business.title: JSW Steel’s Q1 profit soars 2x YoY on robust topline growth
business.summary: JSW Steel's profit more than doubled year-on-year to 4,696 crore in the June quarter. Robust revenue growth and higher volumes aided the company's strong financial performance. Consolidated revenue from operations rose around ten percent on year to 47,364 crore. Total expenses grew less than four percent on year, which helped profitability. The company's net debt also saw a significant reduction during the period.

business.title: US visa rule changes: MEA spokesperson Jaiswal says India will try to ‘minimise difficulties’ for its people
business.summary: The regulatory shifts are projected to heavily influence the demographic of Indian students and academic visitors in the US

business.title: Wall Street's chip index enters bear market! Is the AI bubble finally going bust?
business.summary: Wall Street's semiconductor index slipped into bear market territory after tumbling more than 20% from its June peak, raising fresh questions over the sustainability of the AI-driven rally. A new AI model from China's Moonshot and concerns over heavy AI spending triggered a broad selloff across global technology stocks.

business.title: BSE, Sona BLW among 10 midcap stocks that rose up to 53% in 2026. Do you own any?
business.summary: Midcap stocks have staged a strong comeback in 2026, with Oracle Financial Services, Solar Industries and BSE delivering gains of up to 53% as market sentiment and earnings outlook improved.

business.title: Fairfax picks up half of 3-year government bonds at auction
business.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings likely purchased government bonds as a prelude to acquiring IDBI Bank.  This marks the company's second significant bond purchase in recent months.  The Canadian firm is reportedly preparing to invest capital for a potential IDBI Bank stake.  Fairfax aims to secure a major stake in the government-owned lender.  The IDBI Bank privatisation process has been ongoing since October 2022.

business.title: India's first private rocket to place tech payloads, postcard from PM into orbit
business.summary: Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 launch marks the entry of India’s private space sector into the orbital launch market

business.title: NIfty IT logs best weekly gains since Oct 2025
business.summary: Indian IT stocks experienced a significant rebound this week.  The Nifty IT index saw its best weekly gain since October 2025.  This rally followed a sell-off in Asian AI and semiconductor stocks.  First-quarter earnings from major IT firms showed minimal AI disruption.  Investor confidence improved, leading to some short-covering in the sector.

business.title: Reliance Retail's Q1 profit drops 14% YoY but revenue rises 8%
business.summary: Reliance Retail Ventures reported a fourteen percent profit decline last quarter. Revenue from operations grew eight point two percent year-on-year. Investments in e-commerce and digital infrastructure pressured operating margins. The company aims to double operating Ebitda over three years. Reliance Retail expanded its store network to over twenty thousand locations.

business.title: D-Street bucks Asian markets' meltdown
business.summary: The NSE’s Nifty 50 rose 261.55 points, or 1.1%, to close at 24,334.3, while the BSE Sensex gained 964.58 points, or 1.25%, to end at 78,151.45.

business.title: Investors take to gold dip as Mideast war rages on
business.summary: Gold prices are experiencing their steepest weekly decline in six weeks. This drop has encouraged investors to return to the yellow metal. Consumers are also returning to jewelry stores after recent price reductions. Investors are showing caution towards silver, reducing their exposure. Gold remains a preferred core portfolio asset for many investors.

business.title: RIL's Q1 operational revenue hits milestone with 25% YoY jump
business.summary: Reliance Industries saw a twenty-five percent profit drop in the June quarter.  Revenue from operations increased twenty-five percent, crossing three lakh crore rupees.  Reliance Retail's net profit declined fourteen percent while revenue grew eight percent.  The oil-to-chemicals segment revenue rose over thirty percent amid market volatility.  Jio Platforms reported its first profit decline due to 5G asset capitalization.
